What we do

Facilities

Purpose-built spaces for studying real animal flight and translating those insights into bird-informed aircraft.

Center for Animal Locomotion and Innovation (CALI)

Co-directed with Dr. Michelle Hawkins · California Raptor Center, UC Davis

CALI is a purpose-built flight hall for studying real animal flight and translating those insights into agile aerial robots. We combine controlled indoor flight trials with high-speed motion capture, photogrammetry, and rapid prototyping to test bio-informed designs. The facility, established with a $2.9M DURIP equipment award and opened in 2026, anchors a broader consortium connecting engineering, biology, and veterinary medicine.

Visit the CALI consortium →

Capabilities

  • Controlled-environment indoor flight hall
  • Four attached mews
  • 48-camera OptiTrack Versa 120 system
  • 9-camera high speed Phantom photogrammetry system

UC Davis Aeronautical Wind Tunnel

Co-directed with Dr. Camli Badrya · UC Davis Bainer Hall

A university wind-tunnel facility for precision aerodynamic testing of airfoils, morphing wings, and small UAV platforms. Researchers measure forces, pressures, and flow behavior to validate models and refine designs.

Capabilities

  • Force and pressure measurement
  • Airfoil, morphing-wing & small-UAV testing
  • 48" x 33.6" x 12' test section

Specimen-focused Motion Capture Room

UC Davis Bainer Hall

A dedicated space for high-resolution motion capture and morphological measurement of wing specimens across their full range of motion.

Capabilities

  • Specimen range-of-motion capture
  • Morphological measurement
  • five-camera OptiTrack Flex13 system

Interested in collaborating or visiting? Get in touch.

Bio-Informed Research & Design Lab

Department of Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering, UC Davis, Davis, CA
